SEARCH
Package neqsim.thermo.util.amines

Class AmineKentEisenberg

java.lang.Object
neqsim.thermo.util.amines.AmineKentEisenberg
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class AmineKentEisenberg extends Object implements Serializable
Validated Kent-Eisenberg CO2 solubility model for aqueous alkanolamine solutions.

Implements the apparent-equilibrium-constant (Kent-Eisenberg, 1976) speciation model for the equilibrium CO2 partial pressure over loaded amine solutions. The model solves the coupled carbonate, water, amine-protonation, and (for primary/secondary amines) carbamate equilibria together with the charge, carbon, and amine balances, then applies Henry's law to the free molecular CO2 concentration:

$$ p_{CO_2} = H_{CO_2}(T) \cdot [CO_2]_{free} $$

Carbonate and water dissociation constants are the molality-based correlations of Edwards, Maurer, Newman and Prausnitz (AIChE J., 1978), and the physical CO2 Henry's constant is the Versteeg and van Swaaij (1988) correlation. The amine protonation constants (and, for MEA/DEA, the carbamate reversion constants) are apparent constants tuned so the model reproduces the classic vapour-liquid-equilibrium datasets of Jou, Mather and Otto (Can. J. Chem. Eng., 1982, for MDEA) and Jou, Mather and Otto / Lee, Otto and Mather (for MEA).

The model is intended as a robust screening correlation. Like all Kent-Eisenberg implementations it is most accurate in the engineering loading window (roughly 0.2-0.5 mol/mol for primary and secondary amines, 0.1-1.0 mol/mol for tertiary amines) where its CO2 partial pressure is typically within a factor of about two of the data. It does not resolve activity-coefficient or ionic-strength effects rigorously; for that use the electrolyte equation-of-state path exposed by AmineSystem.getCO2PartialPressureRigorous().

Supported amines and validation status
Amine Class Carbamate Validation
MDEA tertiary no regression-tested vs Jou et al. (1982)
MEA primary yes regression-tested vs Jou/Lee et al.
DEA secondary yes screening only (constants not regression-tuned)
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim

    amineMolarity(double massFraction, double amineMolarMass)
    Estimates the amine molarity (mol/L) of an aqueous solution from its mass fraction.

    freeCO2AtH(double h, double k1, double k2, double kam, double kc, double amineMolarity, double carbonTotal)
    Solves the carbon balance for the free molecular CO2 concentration at a fixed hydrogen-ion concentration for a carbamate-forming amine.
    private static double
    henryCO2(double temperatureK)
    Physical CO2 Henry's constant in water, Versteeg and van Swaaij (1988).
    private static double
    kAmine(AmineKentEisenberg.AmineType type, double temperatureK)
    Apparent amine protonation (dissociation) constant, AmineH+ = Amine + H+.
    private static double
    kCarbamate(AmineKentEisenberg.AmineType type, double temperatureK)
    Apparent carbamate reversion constant, AmineCOO- + H2O = Amine + HCO3-.
    private static double
    kCO2(double temperatureK)
    CO2 hydration constant, CO2 + H2O = H+ + HCO3-, Edwards et al. (1978), molality basis.
    private static double
    kHCO3(double temperatureK)
    Bicarbonate dissociation constant, HCO3- = H+ + CO3--, Edwards et al. (1978), molality basis.
    private static double
    kWater(double temperatureK)
    Water dissociation constant, H2O = H+ + OH-, Edwards et al. (1978), molality basis.

    Calculates the equilibrium CO2 partial pressure over a loaded amine solution.

    Solves the speciation for a primary or secondary amine (MEA, DEA) including carbamate.

    solveTertiary(AmineKentEisenberg.AmineType type, double temperatureK, double amineMolarity, double loading)
    Solves the speciation for a tertiary amine (MDEA, no carbamate).

  • Constructor Details

    • AmineKentEisenberg

      private AmineKentEisenberg()
      Private constructor; the model is used through its static methods.

  • Method Details

    • kCO2

      private static double kCO2(double temperatureK)
      CO2 hydration constant, CO2 + H2O = H+ + HCO3-, Edwards et al. (1978), molality basis.
      Parameters:
      temperatureK - temperature in Kelvin (must be positive)
      Returns:
      equilibrium constant (mol/kg)

    • kHCO3

      private static double kHCO3(double temperatureK)
      Bicarbonate dissociation constant, HCO3- = H+ + CO3--, Edwards et al. (1978), molality basis.
      Parameters:
      temperatureK - temperature in Kelvin (must be positive)
      Returns:
      equilibrium constant (mol/kg)

    • kWater

      private static double kWater(double temperatureK)
      Water dissociation constant, H2O = H+ + OH-, Edwards et al. (1978), molality basis.
      Parameters:
      temperatureK - temperature in Kelvin (must be positive)
      Returns:
      ion product of water (mol/kg)^2

    • henryCO2

      private static double henryCO2(double temperatureK)
      Physical CO2 Henry's constant in water, Versteeg and van Swaaij (1988).

      Returns the Henry coefficient in kPa.L/mol so that the CO2 partial pressure in kPa equals the coefficient multiplied by the free molecular CO2 concentration in mol/L.

      Parameters:
      temperatureK - temperature in Kelvin (must be positive)
      Returns:
      Henry coefficient in kPa.L/mol

    • kAmine

      private static double kAmine(AmineKentEisenberg.AmineType type, double temperatureK)
      Apparent amine protonation (dissociation) constant, AmineH+ = Amine + H+.
      Parameters:
      type - amine type (must not be null)
      temperatureK - temperature in Kelvin (must be positive)
      Returns:
      apparent dissociation constant (mol/L)

    • kCarbamate

      private static double kCarbamate(AmineKentEisenberg.AmineType type, double temperatureK)
      Apparent carbamate reversion constant, AmineCOO- + H2O = Amine + HCO3-.

      A larger value means a less stable carbamate. Tertiary amines (MDEA) do not form carbamate and return Double.NaN.

      Parameters:
      type - amine type (must not be null)
      temperatureK - temperature in Kelvin (must be positive)
      Returns:
      apparent carbamate reversion constant (mol/L), or NaN for tertiary amines

    • amineMolarity

      public static double amineMolarity(double massFraction, double amineMolarMass)
      Estimates the amine molarity (mol/L) of an aqueous solution from its mass fraction.

      Uses a simple linear solution-density estimate, rho [g/L] = 1000 + 80 * massFraction, which reproduces typical alkanolamine solution densities (about 1.04 g/mL for 50 wt% MDEA and about 1.02 g/mL for 30 wt% MEA) closely enough for the apparent-constant model.

      Parameters:
      massFraction - amine mass fraction of the aqueous solution (0 to 1)
      amineMolarMass - amine molar mass in g/mol (must be positive)
      Returns:
      amine concentration in mol/L

    • partialPressureCO2Bara

      public static double partialPressureCO2Bara(AmineKentEisenberg.AmineType type, double temperatureK, double amineMolarity, double loading)
      Calculates the equilibrium CO2 partial pressure over a loaded amine solution.
      Parameters:
      type - amine type (must not be null)
      temperatureK - temperature in Kelvin (must be positive)
      amineMolarity - total amine concentration in mol/L (must be positive)
      loading - CO2 loading in mol CO2 per mol amine (must be non-negative)
      Returns:
      equilibrium CO2 partial pressure in bara

    • solveTertiary

      private static double solveTertiary(AmineKentEisenberg.AmineType type, double temperatureK, double amineMolarity, double loading)
      Solves the speciation for a tertiary amine (MDEA, no carbamate).
      Parameters:
      type - amine type (must not be null)
      temperatureK - temperature in Kelvin (must be positive)
      amineMolarity - total amine concentration in mol/L (must be positive)
      loading - CO2 loading in mol CO2 per mol amine (must be positive)
      Returns:
      CO2 partial pressure in kPa

    • tertiaryResidual

      private static double tertiaryResidual(double h, double k1, double k2, double kw, double kam, double amineMolarity, double carbonTotal)
      Charge-balance residual for the tertiary-amine speciation as a function of hydrogen-ion concentration.
      Parameters:
      h - hydrogen-ion concentration in mol/L (must be positive)
      k1 - CO2 hydration constant
      k2 - bicarbonate dissociation constant
      kw - water ion product
      kam - amine dissociation constant
      amineMolarity - total amine concentration in mol/L
      carbonTotal - total carbon concentration in mol/L
      Returns:
      charge-balance residual (mol/L)

    • solveCarbamate

      private static double solveCarbamate(AmineKentEisenberg.AmineType type, double temperatureK, double amineMolarity, double loading)
      Solves the speciation for a primary or secondary amine (MEA, DEA) including carbamate.
      Parameters:
      type - amine type (must not be null)
      temperatureK - temperature in Kelvin (must be positive)
      amineMolarity - total amine concentration in mol/L (must be positive)
      loading - CO2 loading in mol CO2 per mol amine (must be positive)
      Returns:
      CO2 partial pressure in kPa

    • carbamateResidual

      private static double carbamateResidual(double h, double k1, double k2, double kw, double kam, double kc, double amineMolarity, double carbonTotal)
      Charge-balance residual for the carbamate-forming amine speciation as a function of hydrogen-ion concentration.
      Parameters:
      h - hydrogen-ion concentration in mol/L (must be positive)
      k1 - CO2 hydration constant
      k2 - bicarbonate dissociation constant
      kw - water ion product
      kam - amine dissociation constant
      kc - carbamate reversion constant
      amineMolarity - total amine concentration in mol/L
      carbonTotal - total carbon concentration in mol/L
      Returns:
      charge-balance residual (mol/L)

    • freeCO2AtH

      private static double freeCO2AtH(double h, double k1, double k2, double kam, double kc, double amineMolarity, double carbonTotal)
      Solves the carbon balance for the free molecular CO2 concentration at a fixed hydrogen-ion concentration for a carbamate-forming amine.
      Parameters:
      h - hydrogen-ion concentration in mol/L (must be positive)
      k1 - CO2 hydration constant
      k2 - bicarbonate dissociation constant
      kam - amine dissociation constant
      kc - carbamate reversion constant
      amineMolarity - total amine concentration in mol/L
      carbonTotal - total carbon concentration in mol/L
      Returns:
      free molecular CO2 concentration in mol/L
